name: Y Combinator
funding: $500K investment ($125K for 7% plus $375K on uncapped SAFE)
description: Y Combinator is one of the world's most prominent startup accelerators, running two batches per year in San Francisco. It provides seed funding, intensive mentorship, and access to a vast alumni network.

name: Techstars
funding: $120K investment ($20K for 6% equity plus $100K convertible note)
description: Techstars operates accelerator programs worldwide, partnering with corporations, cities, and universities. Each program runs 13 weeks with mentorship-driven growth and Demo Day exposure.

name: 500 Global
funding: $150K seed investment for accelerator cohorts
description: 500 Global (formerly 500 Startups) is a venture capital firm and accelerator investing in early-stage startups globally. It runs flagship programs in San Francisco and regional programs across continents.

name: Antler
funding: Pre-seed investment from $100K–$250K depending on location
description: Antler is a global early-stage VC and startup generator that helps founders form teams and build companies from scratch. Programs run in 30+ cities across six continents.

name: Plug and Play Tech Center
funding: Corporate pilot opportunities; selective direct investment
description: Plug and Play is a global innovation platform connecting startups with corporations for pilots and partnerships. It runs vertical-specific accelerator programs in Silicon Valley and worldwide.

name: Entrepreneur First
funding: Pre-seed investment up to £80K plus follow-on access
description: Entrepreneur First (EF) is a talent investor that backs individuals before they have a team or idea. It operates in London, Paris, Berlin, Bangalore, Singapore, and Toronto.

name: MassChallenge
funding: Non-dilutive prizes up to $100K; no equity taken
description: MassChallenge is a global network of zero-equity startup accelerators supporting high-impact entrepreneurs. Programs run in Boston, Switzerland, Mexico, Israel, and other locations.

name: Google for Startups
funding: Cloud credits, mentorship, and partner grants (varies by program)
description: Google for Startups provides programs, products, and connections to help startups build and scale. Offerings include Accelerator programs, Cloud credits, and regional hubs worldwide.

name: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub
funding: Up to $150K Azure credits plus access to GitHub Enterprise and OpenAI
description: Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub offers free Azure cloud credits, development tools, and go-to-market support for eligible startups. No equity is required to participate.

name: NVIDIA Inception
funding: GPU credits, co-marketing, and technical support (no direct cash)
description: NVIDIA Inception is a free virtual accelerator program for AI and data-science startups. Members receive GPU credits, technical resources, and access to NVIDIA's investor network.

name: Creative Destruction Lab
funding: No direct funding; access to mentors and investor network
description: Creative Destruction Lab (CDL) is a nonprofit objectives-based program for massively scalable science and technology ventures. Streams include AI, Health, Climate, and Quantum at universities globally.

name: Level39
funding: Workspace and corporate connections; no standard equity investment
description: Level39 is Europe's largest fintech accelerator located in Canary Wharf, London. It provides workspace, events, and connections to financial institutions and investors.

name: Seedcamp
funding: €100K–€200K initial seed investment
description: Seedcamp is Europe's seed fund identifying and investing in world-class founders at the earliest stages. It provides capital, mentorship, and a global network of operators.

name: Startupbootcamp
funding: €15K living stipend plus potential follow-on investment
description: Startupbootcamp runs industry-focused accelerator programs across Europe, Asia-Pacific, and the Americas. Each program lasts three months with corporate partner access.

name: gener8tor
funding: $100K investment for 7.5% equity
description: gener8tor is a nationally ranked accelerator and venture capital firm operating gBETA free accelerators and equity investment programs across the United States.

name: AngelPad
funding: $120K for 7% equity
description: AngelPad is a seed-stage accelerator program in San Francisco and New York City focused on B2B and SaaS startups. It runs intensive 10-week programs twice per year.

name: Hub71
funding: Incentive packages up to AED 750K in subsidies and benefits
description: Hub71 is Abu Dhabi's global tech ecosystem enabling startups to scale through access to capital, corporate partners, and a vibrant community in the UAE capital.

name: Start-Up Chile
funding: CLP 15M–25M equity-free grants depending on program track
description: Start-Up Chile is a government-backed accelerator that attracts early-stage startups worldwide to incorporate and grow in Chile. It offers equity-free funding and a vibrant founder community.

name: Station F
funding: Program-dependent; F/ L.A.B and partner programs offer various support
description: Station F is the world's largest startup campus in Paris, housing hundreds of startups and multiple accelerator programs including Founders Program and corporate labs.

name: Wayra
funding: Up to €150K investment plus Telefónica partnership opportunities
description: Wayra is Telefónica's open innovation hub investing in and accelerating startups across Europe and Latin America. It connects startups with Telefónica business units for pilots.

name: Accelerator Centre
funding: AC:Inc program offers seed investment; other tracks provide mentorship
description: The Accelerator Centre in Waterloo, Canada supports tech startups through structured programs from idea validation to scaling. It is one of Canada's top-ranked incubators.

name: DMZ at Toronto Metropolitan University
funding: Non-dilutive support, workspace, and investor introductions
description: DMZ is a leading startup incubator at Toronto Metropolitan University helping tech founders build and scale companies. It has incubated over 500 startups since 2010.

name: Brinc
funding: Up to $150K investment plus hardware prototyping support
description: Brinc is a global venture accelerator focused on IoT, robotics, food technology, and connected hardware startups. It operates programs in Hong Kong, Bahrain, Poland, and other locations.

name: Flat6Labs
funding: Seed investment up to $500K through follow-on funds
description: Flat6Labs is the MENA region's leading seed and early-stage investor, running accelerator programs in Cairo, Riyadh, Abu Dhabi, Tunis, and other cities.

name: Village Capital
funding: Peer-selected investment up to $100K–$200K
description: Village Capital supports early-stage entrepreneurs solving global problems in finance, health, education, and energy. Its peer-selection model lets founders choose investments.

name: AWS Activate
funding: Up to $100K AWS credits through Activate Portfolio tier
description: AWS Activate provides startups with free tools, resources, and AWS credits to quickly get started on Amazon Web Services. Tiers range from Self-Starter to Portfolio based on affiliation.

name: Betaworks
funding: Camp and accelerator investments from $250K–$500K
description: Betaworks is a New York-based startup studio and early-stage investor building and investing in companies at the intersection of media, AI, and consumer technology.

name: Springboard Enterprises
funding: Access to investor network; alumni have raised $12B+ collectively
description: Springboard Enterprises is a global accelerator for women-led companies seeking equity capital. Its programs connect founders with investors, mentors, and industry experts.

name: Launch Academy
funding: Mentorship-driven program with investor demo day access
description: Launch Academy is a Vancouver-based tech incubator providing education, mentorship, and community for early-stage startups. It has supported 6,000+ entrepreneurs.

name: Berkeley SkyDeck
funding: $200K investment via SkyDeck Fund for accelerator cohort
description: Berkeley SkyDeck is the official startup accelerator of UC Berkeley combining a accelerator program with a dedicated venture fund. It leverages university research and Silicon Valley networks.

name: MIT delta v
funding: $20K stipend plus $2K per team member; no equity taken
description: MIT delta v (formerly Global Founders' Skills Accelerator) is MIT's capstone educational accelerator for student-founded startups. It provides intensive summer programming and MIT resources.

name: StartX
funding: Non-profit accelerator; no equity or fees required
description: StartX is a non-profit startup accelerator for Stanford-affiliated founders. It provides office space, mentorship, and community without taking equity or charging fees.

name: Harvard Innovation Labs
funding: Venture Incubation Program offers workspace and mentorship
description: Harvard Innovation Labs (i-lab) supports Harvard students and alumni in building ventures across all industries. The Venture Incubation Program is its flagship accelerator track.

name: Alchemist Accelerator
funding: $25K seed investment plus Demo Day investor access
description: Alchemist Accelerator is the leading accelerator for enterprise startups that monetize from day one. It focuses exclusively on B2B companies with a structured six-month program.

name: Founders Factory
funding: £30K–£50K initial investment plus studio and corporate support
description: Founders Factory is a global venture studio and accelerator partnering with corporates to build and scale startups. Verticals include fintech, health, and beauty.

name: Capital Factory
funding: Center of gravity for Texas startups; selective angel investments
description: Capital Factory is the center of gravity for entrepreneurs in Texas, providing co-working, events, mentorship, and access to investors. It is one of the most active angel groups in the US.

name: Entrepreneurs Roundtable Accelerator
funding: $100K for 8% equity
description: Entrepreneurs Roundtable Accelerator (ERA) is New York City's leading technology accelerator providing capital, mentorship, and office space. It runs two four-month programs annually.

name: m:lab East Africa
funding: Incubation support and mobile-focused innovation grants
description: m:lab East Africa is a consortium that identifies and supports mobile innovation startups across Kenya and the region. It provides incubation, testing, and investor linkages.

name: Co-Creation Hub (CcHUB)
funding: Design-led incubation with access to CcHUB Design Lab and investors
description: Co-Creation Hub (CcHUB) is Nigeria's first open living lab and pre-incubation space focused on social innovation. It has expanded to Nairobi and Kigali.

name: Endeavor
funding: Mentorship and network access; portfolio companies raise follow-on capital
description: Endeavor selects, mentors, and accelerates high-impact entrepreneurs worldwide. It focuses on scale-stage founders in emerging and growth markets across 40+ countries.

name: SOSV
funding: $500K total investment ($200K program fee plus $300K SAFE)
description: SOSV is a multi-stage venture fund running vertical accelerators including HAX (hardware), IndieBio (biotech), and Food-X (food tech). It invests in 150+ startups annually.

name: Neo Accelerator
funding: Investment from associated Neo fund for selected founders
description: Neo is a mentorship community and accelerator for exceptional engineers and founders. It combines a competitive accelerator program with a venture fund backing technical founders.

name: Founder Institute
funding: Equity collective model; graduates join shared equity pool
description: Founder Institute is the world's largest pre-seed accelerator, operating in 100+ cities globally. Its part-time four-month program helps founders launch meaningful and enduring companies.

name: Techstars London
funding: $120K investment standard Techstars deal
description: Techstars London is a flagship Techstars accelerator based in the UK capital. It connects startups with London's fintech ecosystem, mentors, and European investor networks.

name: Chinaccelerator
funding: Part of MOX accelerator; access to Asia mobile markets
description: Chinaccelerator is a mentorship-driven accelerator helping startups access Asian markets, particularly mobile-first economies. It is operated by SOSV as part of the MOX program.

name: D.CAMP
funding: Banks Foundation-backed support and investor matching
description: D.CAMP is Korea's leading startup hub operated by Banks Foundation for Young Entrepreneurs, providing workspace, programs, and community for Korean and international startups.

name: Launch Pad Technology Business Incubator
funding: Western Sydney University incubator with grants and mentorship
description: Launch Pad is Western Sydney University's innovation hub supporting startups with co-working, mentoring, and connections to research. It serves the Greater Western Sydney ecosystem.

name: Startmate
funding: $120K AUD investment for 7.5% equity
description: Startmate is Australia and New Zealand's leading accelerator backing ambitious founders building global companies. It runs two cohorts per year with intensive mentorship.

name: Buildit Accelerator
funding: Up to €50K investment for hardware and IoT startups
description: Buildit is the Baltic region's first accelerator focused on hardware, IoT, and physical product startups. It provides prototyping labs and manufacturing connections.

name: Parallel18
funding: $40K equity-free grant for accelerator cohort
description: Parallel18 is a top-level accelerator in Puerto Rico supporting global startups with equity-free grants and access to the US and Latin American markets.

name: T-Hub
funding: Lab32 accelerator offers seed funding; various corporate programs
description: T-Hub is India's largest innovation hub based in Hyderabad, supporting startups through incubation, acceleration, and corporate innovation programs. It has supported 2,000+ startups.

name: CIIE.CO
funding: Seed fund and accelerator investments up to ₹1 Cr
description: CIIE.CO (IIM Ahmedabad's Centre for Innovation Incubation and Entrepreneurship) is one of India's pioneering incubators. It runs accelerators, seed funds, and Bharat Inclusion initiatives.

name: NSRCEL
funding: Launchpad and incubation programs with IIMB seed support
description: NSRCEL at IIM Bangalore is a leading startup hub offering incubation, acceleration, and entrepreneurship education. It supports startups from idea stage through early growth.

name: Society for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (SINE)
funding: Seed support and government grant facilitation
description: SINE IIT Bombay is one of India's first university incubators, supporting technology startups spun out of IIT Bombay research. It provides incubation space, mentoring, and funding access.

name: AIC Banasthali Vidyapith Foundation
funding: Atal Innovation Mission grant-backed incubation support
description: AIC Banasthali is an Atal Incubation Centre at Banasthali Vidyapith supporting women entrepreneurs and startups from Rajasthan and beyond with incubation facilities and mentorship.

name: Kerala Startup Mission
funding: Seed fund, innovation grants, and soft loan schemes
description: Kerala Startup Mission (KSUM) is the nodal agency for entrepreneurship development in Kerala. It operates multiple incubators, a seed fund, and the Huddle Global conference.

name: iCreate (International Centre for Entrepreneurship and Technology)
funding: Equity and grant funding up to ₹25 lakh through various programs
description: iCreate is an autonomous technology business incubator in Gujarat focused on deep tech, hardware, and IP-led startups. It is supported by the Government of Gujarat and Government of India.

name: IIT Madras Incubation Cell
funding: Seed grants and access to IIT Madras research infrastructure
description: IIT Madras Incubation Cell (RTBI and IITMIC) supports startups leveraging IIT Madras research and talent. It is among India's most prolific academic incubators by startup count.

name: iHub Hyderabad
funding: Telangana government-backed incubation and prototyping grants
description: iHub Hyderabad is a deeptech-focused incubator supported by the Telangana government and DST. It provides prototyping labs, mentorship, and connections to research institutions.

name: Forge Innovation & Ventures
funding: Accelerator investments and manufacturing support programs
description: Forge is a Coimbatore-based accelerator and incubator focused on hardware, manufacturing, and industrial startups. It bridges the gap between prototype and production.

name: Zone Startups India
funding: Corporate accelerator programs with pilot and investment opportunities
description: Zone Startups India is a Toronto-based accelerator's Mumbai outpost running corporate-linked accelerator programs. It connects startups with enterprise partners for validation and pilots.

name: IIM Calcutta Innovation Park
funding: Incubation support and access to IIM Calcutta network
description: IIM Calcutta Innovation Park (IIMCIP) promotes entrepreneurship in Eastern India through incubation, acceleration, and innovation programs linked to IIM Calcutta.

name: NASSCOM Center of Excellence
funding: CoE programs with MeitY support; access to corporate labs
description: NASSCOM Center of Excellence for IoT and AI supports startups through labs, corporate co-creation, and market access. Centers operate in Bangalore, Gurugram, Ahmedabad, and Vizag.

name: AIC IIIT Hyderabad Foundation
funding: AIM grant-backed incubation and prototyping support
description: AIC at IIIT Hyderabad is an Atal Incubation Centre leveraging the institute's AI and computer science expertise. It incubates startups in deep tech and software products.

name: AIC Pimpri Chinchwad College of Engineering
funding: Atal Innovation Mission supported incubation grants
description: AIC PCMC is an Atal Incubation Centre in Pune's industrial hub supporting hardware, manufacturing, and engineering startups with lab facilities and industry mentorship.

name: AIC BIMTECH
funding: AIM-backed incubation with seed grant access
description: AIC BIMTECH is an Atal Incubation Centre at BIMTECH Greater Noida supporting early-stage startups with workspace, mentoring, and investor connect programs.

name: AIC JK Lakshmipat University
funding: Incubation support under Atal Innovation Mission
description: AIC JK Lakshmipat University in Jaipur provides incubation facilities and entrepreneurship programs for Rajasthan-based startups through the Atal Innovation Mission framework.

name: AIC Anna University Incubation Foundation
funding: AIM grants and Tamil Nadu startup policy benefits
description: AIC Anna University supports technology startups in Tamil Nadu with incubation space, prototyping labs, and connections to one of India's largest engineering universities.

name: Rural Technology Business Incubator (RTBI)
funding: DST and IIT Madras supported seed grants for social ventures
description: RTBI at IIT Madras incubates startups addressing rural and social challenges in agriculture, healthcare, and livelihoods. It is one of India's pioneering social incubators.

name: AIC GITAM University
funding: Atal Innovation Mission incubation funding
description: AIC GITAM is an Atal Incubation Centre at GITAM University supporting startups from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana with incubation, mentoring, and funding facilitation.

name: Startup India Hub
funding: Fund of Funds, seed fund scheme, and tax benefit facilitation
description: Startup India Hub is the Government of India's flagship platform connecting startups with funding, mentorship, and compliance support. It administers the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme.

name: AIC Chhattisgarh (NIT Raipur)
funding: AIM-supported incubation with state startup incentives
description: AIC Chhattisgarh at NIT Raipur supports startups from central India with incubation facilities, mentorship, and access to Atal Innovation Mission resources.

name: Electropreneur Park
funding: MeitY and STPI supported ESDM incubation grants
description: Electropreneur Park is an ESDM-focused incubator in Delhi supported by MeitY and STPI. It provides electronics prototyping labs and mentorship for hardware startups.

name: AIC Shaheed Sukhdev College of Business Studies
funding: AIM incubation grants and Delhi startup ecosystem access
description: AIC SSCBS is a Delhi University-affiliated Atal Incubation Centre supporting student and alumni startups with workspace, mentoring, and early-stage funding connections.

name: Society for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (SINE) DU
funding: University of Delhi incubation support and grant facilitation
description: SINE at Delhi University incubates startups founded by DU students, faculty, and alumni. It provides co-working space and entrepreneurship training on campus.

name: AIC Nalanda Institute of Technology Foundation
funding: Atal Innovation Mission incubation support
description: AIC Nalanda in Bhubaneswar supports Odisha-based startups through the Atal Incubation Centre program with workspace, mentoring, and investor networking.

name: Ahmedabad University Incubator
funding: University-backed incubation and Gujarat startup scheme access
description: Ahmedabad University Incubator supports interdisciplinary ventures from students and faculty with mentorship, workspace, and connections to Gujarat's startup ecosystem.

name: AIC Bhansali & Co
funding: AIM grants plus angel network access via Bhansali & Co
description: AIC Bhansali & Co in Hyderabad combines Atal Incubation Centre infrastructure with active angel investor networks, supporting early-stage startups in Telangana.

name: Agri Business Incubation Centre IIT Kharagpur
funding: ICAR and DST supported agtech incubation grants
description: The Agri Business Incubation Centre at IIT Kharagpur supports agritech and food-processing startups with labs, field testing, and connections to agricultural research.

name: MeitY Startup Hub
funding: TIDE 2.0, SAMRIDH, and other MeitY scheme funding
description: MeitY Startup Hub is the Ministry of Electronics and IT's platform coordinating startup schemes including TIDE centers, EIR programs, and the SAMRIDH accelerator.

name: IndieBio
funding: $525K total ($250K program fee plus $275K SAFE)
description: IndieBio is the world's largest biotech startup accelerator, run by SOSV. It helps scientists build biotech companies with lab space, mentorship, and seed capital in San Francisco.

name: HAX
funding: $500K total investment package via SOSV
description: HAX is SOSV's hard tech accelerator providing hardware startups with prototyping labs, supply chain expertise, and seed funding. Programs run in Newark and Shenzhen.

name: FinTech Sandbox
funding: Free access to data feeds and APIs from 40+ partners
description: FinTech Sandbox provides startups with free access to financial data and infrastructure for building and testing products. It operates in Boston and San Francisco.

name: BioNEST (BIRAC)
funding: BIRAC seed fund, BIG grants up to ₹50 lakh, and lab access
description: BioNEST is BIRAC's bio-incubation program supporting life-sciences startups across 50+ centers in India. It provides shared labs, equipment, and biotech-specific mentoring.

name: Rock Health
funding: Digital health advisory and capital advisory services
description: Rock Health is a seed fund and advisory firm dedicated exclusively to digital health startups. It provides market insights, advisory services, and investor connections.

name: MATTER
funding: Corporate partnership programs and investor showcase events
description: MATTER is a healthcare startup incubator and innovation center in Chicago connecting startups with hospitals, pharma companies, and payers for pilots and partnerships.

name: Imagine H2O
funding: Non-dilutive prizes and access to water industry partners
description: Imagine H2O is a nonprofit water innovation accelerator supporting startups addressing global water challenges. Its annual competition provides funding and industry visibility.

name: Food-X
funding: $500K SOSV investment package for food tech cohorts
description: Food-X is SOSV's accelerator for food technology and alternative protein startups. It provides kitchen labs, industry mentors, and seed funding in New York City.

name: Zeroth.AI
funding: $120K investment for AI accelerator cohorts
description: Zeroth.AI is Asia's first accelerator dedicated to artificial intelligence startups. It provides funding, technical mentorship, and access to corporate AI partners.

name: Dreamit Health
funding: Up to $500K investment for health tech startups
description: Dreamit Health is a growth-focused accelerator for health tech startups with traction. It connects companies with health systems for pilots and commercial partnerships.

name: Cleantech Open
funding: Global Ideas competition prizes and accelerator mentorship
description: Cleantech Open runs the world's largest cleantech accelerator, helping startups develop business models for energy, water, agriculture, and transportation solutions.

name: Greentown Labs
funding: Lab space and accelerator programs; access to climatetech investors
description: Greentown Labs is the largest climatetech startup incubator in North America with locations in Somerville and Houston. It provides lab space and a 200+ company community.

name: FinTech Innovation Lab
funding: 12-week program with bank and insurer pilot opportunities
description: FinTech Innovation Lab London is an accelerator run by Accenture connecting fintech startups with leading banks and insurers for product validation and partnerships.

name: Healthbox
funding: Advisory and corporate innovation programs for health startups
description: Healthbox, part of HIMSS, helps healthcare organizations and startups accelerate innovation through advisory services, accelerator programs, and industry connections.

name: Katapult Ocean
funding: €150K investment for ocean impact accelerator cohorts
description: Katapult Ocean invests in and accelerates startups tackling ocean sustainability challenges including plastic pollution, overfishing, and marine ecosystem health.

name: CyberLaunch
funding: $50K investment plus cybersecurity mentor network access
description: CyberLaunch is a cybersecurity-focused accelerator in Atlanta providing seed funding, mentorship, and connections to enterprise security buyers and investors.

name: MetaProp
funding: PropTech accelerator with $250K investment opportunities
description: MetaProp is the leading PropTech venture capital firm and accelerator investing in startups transforming real estate and the built environment.

name: Leaps by Bayer - Co.Lab
funding: Lab space and venture support through Leaps by Bayer
description: Leaps by Bayer invests in breakthrough innovations in health and agriculture. Its Co.Lab programs provide lab infrastructure and venture support for biotech startups.

name: Starburst Aerospace Accelerator
funding: Corporate partnership programs with aerospace and defense primes
description: Starburst is the first and only global aerospace accelerator connecting startups with Boeing, Airbus, and defense contractors. It runs programs in Los Angeles, Munich, and other hubs.

name: AWS EdStart
funding: Up to $100K AWS credits for qualifying edtech startups
description: AWS EdStart is Amazon Web Services' virtual incubation program for edtech startups. It provides cloud credits, technical training, and community support for education technology companies.
